inline_comments.message: this looks wrong, helm targets order depends on how Makefile is written, this pair is matching ONAP Casablanca case, In Beijing "make all" was sufficient. According to logic described in other places, we should have this empty and set in config/application_configuration.yml example

by moving all of those into group_vars and setting default's there (mainly deriving from app_name) it will limit us in the future. With this commit we are setting artificial dependency on:
app_name ->  helm chart, release, namespace
Maybe it's not bad, people can change group_vars.yml right.
We are using group_vars.yml as some template for user and everyone is supposed to create own file out of this.

Hmm, I removed the extra variable  project_configuration because some people were confused. 
Now there is no extra variables and it's streamlined to the minimum.
This will not limit anybody. And people are not changing group_vars but their own application_configuration.yml (this file) where they can override ANY variable they want.

Yes the design of ansible and also this project has been from the start exactly that, group_vars are "template" with good default values, but user configures over it with own variable file when needed.
